experiment.title: Reverse Token Prediction on 8xH100
experiment.subtitle: RunPod nanochat reverse pretraining run, April 29 2026
launch_log_notes: The surviving zipped logfile preserved launch metadata and the first 201 training steps.
Later validation checkpoints and sampling judgments were reconstructed from the terminal transcript after the remote container was lost.

notes: The run clearly accelerated far beyond the first 200 logged steps after the long setup phase.
The later transcript indicates stable operation around roughly 0.97M tok/sec and about 58-59% BF16 MFU.

manual_probe_scores.label: 3000 default
manual_probe_scores.verdict: First checkpoint that clearly looked non-random and useful for reverse landing.

manual_probe_scores.label: 4000 default
manual_probe_scores.verdict: Some anchors became cleaner, but repetition became much more obvious.

manual_probe_scores.label: 5000 default
manual_probe_scores.verdict: Best balance observed: short exits improved without fully collapsing into loops.

manual_probe_scores.label: 5000 temp0.25 top20
manual_probe_scores.verdict: Too deterministic; the model fell into strong local loops.

manual_probe_scores.label: 5000 temp0.9 top80
manual_probe_scores.verdict: Escaped some loops but hallucinated harder and drifted more.

manual_probe_scores.label: 5000 temp0.7 top50
manual_probe_scores.verdict: Not better than default; still loopy and still factually weak.

qualitative_takeaways: The reverse objective worked: the model usually generated text that landed the requested anchor.
Topic steering improved materially between 3000 and 5000, especially on history anchors.
Factuality remained weak at this scale; the model produced plausible lead-ins rather than truthful reconstructions.
Decoding changes traded loopiness against hallucination. Default 5000 was the best observed compromise.

postmortem.outcome: The run was lost after the remote container restarted at about 99.69 percent completion.
postmortem.root_cause: Checkpoints were written under /root/.cache/nanochat_reverse while the persistent volume and downloaded workspace only covered /workspace.
evidence: The reopened container no longer had /root/.cache.
Searches for model_*.pt, meta_*.json, and optim_*.pt under /root and /workspace returned nothing.
The downloaded workspace zip preserved repo files and the screen logfile but no checkpoints.
retained_artifacts: The source code and launch scripts
The terminal transcript with later validation checkpoints
The CPU probe observations for checkpoints 3000, 4000, and 5000
